rationale: The US-Iran conflict enters its second consecutive day of mutual strikes with a tanker hit in the Gulf of Oman — this is a qualitative continuation with new details (tanker hit, day 2 confirmed) that keeps energy price risk high for Poland, though no major new escalation beyond the established baseline.

top_events.title: US-Iran mutual strikes continue — day 2, tanker hit in Gulf of Oman
top_events.summary: US and Iran exchanged strikes for a second consecutive day; the US reportedly hit a tanker in the Gulf of Oman with three Indian sailors missing. The conflict is now confirmed as sustained, keeping Hormuz closure risk and global oil supply disruption elevated — directly threatening Polish energy and fuel prices.

top_events.title: Pakistan launches deadly air strikes inside Afghanistan
top_events.summary: Pakistan conducted deadly air strikes in Afghanistan, with funerals now being held for victims. This is a continuation of last cycle's flare-up with no qualitative change; a distant regional conflict with negligible direct impact on Poland.

top_events.title: Anti-immigrant unrest in Belfast — police use water cannon
top_events.summary: Anti-immigrant protesters clashed with police in Belfast, torching a car; water cannon deployed. Unrest in a European city is worth noting, but Northern Ireland is geographically and politically distant from Poland with no direct impact on Polish security or daily life.
